questions.q: Which office did Jawaharlal Nehru hold as the first holder in independent India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: In which year did Jawaharlal Nehru become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Nehru took office as Prime Minister in 1947, at the time of independence.

questions.q: In which year did Jawaharlal Nehru's tenure as Prime Minister end?
questions.explanation: Nehru served as Prime Minister until his death in 1964.

questions.q: Which famous speech did Jawaharlal Nehru deliver on the eve of India's independence?
questions.explanation: Nehru delivered the 'Tryst with Destiny' speech on the eve of independence in 1947.

questions.q: Jawaharlal Nehru holds which distinction regarding the office of Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Nehru is the longest-serving Prime Minister of India, holding office from 1947 to 1964.

questions.q: Whose father was Jawaharlal Nehru, among later Prime Ministers of India?
questions.explanation: Nehru was the father of Indira Gandhi, who later became Prime Minister.

questions.q: Children's Day in India is observed on Jawaharlal Nehru's birthday, which falls on which date?
questions.explanation: Nehru's birthday, 14 November, is observed as Children's Day in India.

questions.q: Jawaharlal Nehru was widely known by which affectionate title among children?
questions.explanation: Nehru was popularly known as 'Chacha Nehru', associated with his fondness for children.

questions.q: Besides being Prime Minister, which key cabinet portfolio did Nehru also commonly hold?
questions.explanation: Nehru also held the External Affairs portfolio for much of his tenure as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Nehru led India during the early decades following independence in which year?
questions.explanation: India became independent in 1947, when Nehru assumed the office of Prime Minister.

questions.q: Which institution, set up in 1950 to formulate Five-Year Plans, did Nehru chair as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Nehru, as Prime Minister, was the first Chairman of the Planning Commission established in 1950.

questions.q: Nehru is closely associated with which foreign-policy approach during the Cold War?
questions.explanation: Nehru was a leading proponent of the policy of Non-Alignment in foreign affairs.

questions.q: The principles of 'Panchsheel', associated with Nehru's diplomacy, concerned what?
questions.explanation: Panchsheel referred to the Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence in foreign relations.

questions.q: In how many general elections held during his lifetime did Nehru lead his party to a Lok Sabha majority?
questions.explanation: Nehru led his party in the general elections of 1951-52, 1957 and 1962, three contests in all.

questions.q: Which book did Nehru write in the form of letters to his daughter?
questions.explanation: 'Glimpses of World History' was written by Nehru as a series of letters to his daughter.

questions.q: Which well-known book on India's history and civilisation did Nehru author?
questions.explanation: Nehru authored 'The Discovery of India', a reflection on Indian history and culture.

questions.q: Nehru delivered the 'Tryst with Destiny' speech on the night of which date?
questions.explanation: The speech was delivered on the night of 14-15 August 1947, as India became independent.

questions.q: Nehru championed large public-sector projects such as dams and steel plants, which he described using which phrase?
questions.explanation: Nehru famously called major industrial and infrastructure projects the 'temples of modern India'.

questions.q: Who succeeded Jawaharlal Nehru as Prime Minister of India after his death in 1964?
questions.explanation: Lal Bahadur Shastri became the next regular Prime Minister after Nehru's death in 1964.

questions.q: Before independence, Nehru chaired which body that drafted an early statement of objectives for the Constitution?
options: He moved the Objectives Resolution in the Constituent Assembly
questions.explanation: Nehru moved the Objectives Resolution in the Constituent Assembly, which foreshadowed the Preamble.

questions.q: Nehru's economic vision for India is often described as which kind of model?
options: A mixed economy with a strong public sector
questions.explanation: Nehru favoured a mixed economy in which the state and private sectors both played roles.

questions.q: Nehru served as President of the Indian National Congress at the historic session that adopted which 1929 resolution?
questions.explanation: Nehru presided over the 1929 Lahore session, which adopted the Purna Swaraj (complete independence) resolution.

questions.q: In which month and year did Jawaharlal Nehru pass away while in office?
questions.explanation: Nehru died in May 1964 while serving as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Which premier scientific or research institution network expanded significantly under Nehru's government?
questions.explanation: The Indian Institutes of Technology were established and expanded during Nehru's tenure as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Nehru was a key figure in founding which international grouping of newly independent and developing nations?
questions.explanation: Nehru was among the principal founders of the Non-Aligned Movement.

questions.q: Nehru's autobiography, written largely during a period of imprisonment, is commonly known by which title?
questions.explanation: Nehru's autobiography is titled 'An Autobiography', published abroad as 'Toward Freedom'.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act, which redrew state boundaries largely on linguistic lines, was enacted during Nehru's tenure in which year?
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Act was passed in 1956 while Nehru was Prime Minister.

questions.q: Nehru is credited with shaping India's early commitment to which broad set of national objectives in the Preamble's spirit?
options: A single-party state with no elections
Democracy, secularism and a planned, self-reliant economy
questions.explanation: Nehru's vision emphasised parliamentary democracy, secularism and planned economic self-reliance.

questions.q: Which national academy of letters and other cultural academies were founded under Nehru's government in the 1950s?
options: The Sahitya Akademi and related academies
The Reserve Bank and the CAG
The Supreme Court and High Courts
questions.explanation: The Sahitya Akademi and other national cultural academies were established during Nehru's tenure.

questions.q: Nehru served as the Vice-President of the interim government's Executive Council beginning in which year before becoming Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Nehru headed the interim government formed in 1946 before becoming Prime Minister in 1947.
